LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OAKLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS DENIED. SAN FRANCISCO VENUE IS PROPER UNDER CCP 395 AND CCP 401 BECAUSE THE CAL. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION IS A PARTY TO THIS CASE AND THE ATTORNEY GENERAL HAS AN OFFICE IN SAN FRANCISCO. CCP 394 DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS NOT ALLEGING AN INJURY BASED ON NEGLIGENCE. CCP 393 DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PETITIONER'S PETITION IS BASED ON A FAILURE TO ACT RATHER THAN AFFIRMATIVE CONDUCT. SEE STATE COMMISSION IN LUNACY V. WELCH (1908) 154 CAL. 775, 777. THE MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE BASED ON CCP 397 [CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES] IS PREMATURE BECAUSE THERE IS NO ANSWER ON FILE. SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR CRT., (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 245; BURAN EQUIPMENT CO., INC. V. SUP CRT., (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Oakland Unified School Districts Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OAKLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion To Transfer Venue is denied. San Francisco venue is proper under CCP 395 and CCP 401 because the Cal. Department of Education is a party to this case and the Attorney General has an office in San Francisco. CCP 394 does not apply because plaintiff is not alleging an injury based on negligence. CCP 393 does not apply because Petitioner's petition is based on a failure to act rather than affirmative conduct. See State Commission in Lunacy v. Welch (1908) 154 Cal. 775, 777. The motion to change venue based on CCP 397 [convenience of witnesses] is premature because there is no answer on file. See Easton v. Superior Crt., (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245; Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup Crt., (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
